metadata.scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th Class

raw_data.details: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the “Bharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th Class" for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme is applicable for the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts.
raw_data.benefits: Financial Assistance for the 6th Class to 10th Class: ₹7,000.
raw_data.eligibility: The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. The student should be studying in Andhra Pradesh. The student's parents/guardians should reside in Andhra Pradesh. The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000. The student under the category of orphan, differently abled (physically handicapped), or children of single parents' priority will be given. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS). The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose. However, students benefitting from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme.
raw_data.application_process: OnlineRegistrationStep-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name. Step-03: Fill out the application form with required details like:Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etcUpload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etcFinalize and "Submit" the application. Step-04: Receive the reference number after the submission. Check Application Status:Step-01: Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "[Know your status](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/eSchemes/know_stat.aspx)". Step-02: Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. Step-03: Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details. Download the Acknowledgement slip:Step-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme. Step-03: Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme". Step-04: Click on the "Submit".
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh). White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva). Caste Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Residence Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Birth Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Study Certificate from the School. SB Account Passbook in the name of the Applicant. NOTE: The account should be in Andhra Bank or SBI Bank).

To know more please visit Check EligibilityBharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th ClassAndhra PradeshBharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th ClassBrahminFinancial AssistanceStudentCheck EligibilityDetailsAndhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the “Bharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th Class" for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme is applicable for the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts. BenefitsFinancial Assistance for the 6th Class to 10th Class: ₹7,000. EligibilityThe student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. The student should be studying in Andhra Pradesh. The student's parents/guardians should reside in Andhra Pradesh. The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000. The student under the category of orphan, differently abled (physically handicapped), or children of single parents' priority will be given. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS). The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose. However, students benefitting from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme. Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name. Step-03: Fill out the application form with required details like:Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etcUpload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etcFinalize and "Submit" the application. Step-04: Receive the reference number after the submission. Check Application Status:Step-01: Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "[Know your status](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/eSchemes/know_stat.aspx)". Step-02: Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. Step-03: Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details. Download the Acknowledgement slip:Step-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme. Step-03: Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme". Step-04: Click on the "Submit". Documents RequiredAadhar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh). White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva). Caste Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Residence Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Birth Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Study Certificate from the School. SB Account Passbook in the name of the Applicant. NOTE: The account should be in Andhra Bank or SBI Bank). Frequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible for the Bharati Scheme for Education (BSE)? Brahmin students residing in Andhra Pradesh with family. What is the financial assistance amount for students in 6th to 10th classes? The financial assistance is ₹7,000 for the academic year. Is there a specific income criterion for eligibility? Yes, annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000. Are students already benefiting from other government schemes eligible? No, except for those receiving benefits from the Archaka Welfare Trust. Who processes the applications for the BSE scheme? The State Level Selection Committee (SLSC) of A. P. Brahmin Welfare Corporation. Can students with backlogs in their previous studies apply? No, applicants must have passed all subjects in the previous academic years. Is there any preference given to specific categories of students? Yes, priority is given to orphans, differently abled, and children of single parents. How can students apply for the BSE scheme? They need to register online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation's portal. How is financial assistance disbursed under the BSE? The amount is credited directly to the students' bank accounts. How are eligibility criteria verified for the BSE scheme? Data is cross-checked with Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies departments. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/down/bharathi2019.pdf)

raw_data.scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - 6th Class to 10th Class

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Bharati Scheme for Education (BSE)?
faqs.answer: Brahmin students residing in Andhra Pradesh with family.

faqs.question: What is the financial assistance amount for students in 6th to 10th classes?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance is ₹7,000 for the academic year.

faqs.question: Is there a specific income criterion for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000.

faqs.question: Are students already benefiting from other government schemes eligible?
faqs.answer: No, except for those receiving benefits from the Archaka Welfare Trust.

faqs.question: Who processes the applications for the BSE scheme?
faqs.answer: The State Level Selection Committee (SLSC) of A. P. Brahmin Welfare Corporation.

faqs.question: Can students with backlogs in their previous studies apply?
faqs.answer: No, applicants must have passed all subjects in the previous academic years.

faqs.question: Is there any preference given to specific categories of students?
faqs.answer: Yes, priority is given to orphans, differently abled, and children of single parents.

faqs.question: How can students apply for the BSE scheme?
faqs.answer: They need to register online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation's portal.

faqs.question: How is financial assistance disbursed under the BSE?
faqs.answer: The amount is credited directly to the students' bank accounts.

faqs.question: How are eligibility criteria verified for the BSE scheme?
faqs.answer: Data is cross-checked with Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies departments.
